steps.title: Follow the lighting effect
steps.content: The researchers found that poor street lighting deters new or inexperienced cyclists but has little effect on experienced cyclists. Improving lighting should therefore mainly affect the deterred group.

steps.title: Reject backwards or overbroad effects
steps.content: Choice A goes backward because removing a deterrent should not reduce new cyclists. Choices B and C focus on experienced cyclists, but the passage says lighting has little effect on them.

steps.title: Predict the likely result
steps.content: The remaining choice follows logically: adding streetlights could increase the number of new or inexperienced cyclists riding in the city. The answer is D.
